prs.title: [vLLM IR] 2/N fused_add_rms_norm and maybe_inplace overload
prs.author: ProExpertProg
prs.body_head:
# [vLLM IR] 2/N Add maybe_inplace overload for in-place operation support

## Summary
This PR extends the vLLM IR framework to support optional in-place semantics 
(reusing input activation memory for outputs) via a new `maybe_inplace` overload.
It includes dispatching/registration infrastructure and additional compiler passes
to manage functionalization through the compilation pipeline.
Finally, this PR ports the `fused_add_rms_norm` to IR using the `maybe_inplace` overload.

Key features:
- **`maybe_inplace` overload**: Ops can allow in-place implementations to reuse activation tensor memory
- **eager-mode functionalization**: When the default overload dispatches to an inplace implementation,
activations are cloned to preserve their contents
- **E2E Compilation pipeline integration**: New and existing passes manage IR ops through the compilation pipeline, 
from functionalization to lowering and clone elimination.
- **Fusion passes updated**: Now using the `fused_add_rms_norm` IR op instead of 
- **IR documentation**: Added a full doc on all vLLM IR features: registration, dispatching, compilation, and overall design.
- **Unit & integration tests**: IR infra, kernels, compilation, etc.

## Motivation

vLLM IR separates operation **semantics** from **implementation**, enabling multiple implementations per op.
However, some kernels might optimize memory usage by reusing input activation buffers for outputs.
This poses two problems: (1) torch.compile does not like in-place operations, 
and (2) not all implementations of the IR op will have the same behavior.

To solve this, we introduce the `maybe_inplace` overload, which is allowed (but not required)
to alias output tensors to inputs. When a user calls the `maybe_inplace` overload, it signals
to the dispatcher & compiler that the inputs are no longer needed and their memory may be reused for outputs.

## Changes

### Core IR (`vllm/ir/op.py`)

1. **Extended `@register_op` decorator**

prs.title: [ROCm] Add env flags to disable dynamic MXFP4 quant and enable AITER tuned GEMMs for Attention Projection Layers
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
For models in the DeepSeek family, dynamic MXFP4 quantization is enabled by default for the attention projection layers. This behavior may be suboptimal in certain deployment scenarios.

This PR introduces two opt-in environment variables that provide finer control over this path:

1. The ability to disable dynamic MXFP4 quantization for attention projections.
2. The ability to route unquantized attention GEMMs through AITER's newer FlyDSL-based tuned GEMM implementations, instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

In internal benchmarks, using the FlyDSL GEMM path for unquantized attention projections shows a good performance improvement on models such as Kimi-K2-Thinking.

## Summary
This PR adds two new ROCm environment variables to give operators finer control over quantization and GEMM dispatch for attention linear layers:
- **`VLLM_ROCM_DISABLE_ATTENTION_LINEAR_LAYER_DYNAMIC_MXFP4_QUANT`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, skips the dynamic MXFP4 quantization that is applied to attention projection layers (`q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`) which is done upon model load for a certain family of models.
- **`VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_TUNED_UNQUANTISED_GEMM`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, routes unquantized GEMMs in the attention path through AITER's tuned GEMM implementation (`aiter.tuned_gemm.tgemm.mm`) instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

Both flags are opt-in and default to the current behavior, so there is no change for existing users.

## Test Result
### Accuracy
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

Benchmark | Metric | Score | Threshold | Status
-- | -- | -- | -- | --
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (flexible-extract) | 0.944 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (strict-match) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS

### Performance
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

| config | conc | Baseline | AiterTunedFI |

prs.title: [Model] Add RWKV-7 (Goose) inference support
prs.author: sirus20x6
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Adds [RWKV-7 ("Goose")](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.14456) to vLLM v1, targeting the dense fla-format checkpoints at `fla-hub/rwkv7-{0.1B,0.4B,1.5B,2.9B}-{g1,world}`. RWKV-7 is an attention-free recurrent model: per-sequence per-layer state is a fixed `(num_heads, head_dim, head_v_dim)` fp32 matrix plus two 1-token shift buffers (one for the time-mix block, one for the channel-mix FFN). No KV cache, constant memory regardless of context length.

Resolves #3583 (which was originally requesting RWKV support generally; this PR covers v7, the production version of the family).

This is not a duplicate of #11193, which targeted RWKV-6 with a different state mechanism and was closed/stale.

### What's in the PR

- `vllm/model_executor/models/rwkv7.py` — model, decoder layer, channel-mix, `RWKV7ForCausalLM`. `HasInnerState + IsAttentionFree + SupportsMambaPrefixCaching`.
- `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/rwkv7_mixer.py` — time-mix block. Wraps the kernel call in `torch.ops.vllm.rwkv7_attention` so token-shift, projections, `v_first` lerp, GroupNorm, gate-output correction, and o_proj stay compile-friendly.
- `vllm/v1/attention/backends/rwkv7_attn.py` — thin metadata backend mirroring linear-attn / GDN style. Pure-decode batches use `UNIFORM_SINGLE_TOKEN_DECODE` cudagraphs; mixed batches and prefills route to `chunk_rwkv7`.
- `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/rwkv7/` — vendored forward-only Triton kernels (`chunk`, `fused_recurrent`, plus `wy_fast`/`chunk_h`/`chunk_o`/`chunk_a`/`cumsum` deps) copied from flash-linear-attention with bwd kernels and `Function.apply` wrappers stripped.
- `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/mamba_utils.py` — `rwkv7_state_shape` / `rwkv7_state_dtype` / `rwkv7_state_copy_func` for the two-shift + recurrent triple.
- `vllm/v1/attention/backends/registry.py` — `RWKV7_ATTN` enum + `"rwkv7" -> RWKV7_ATTN` mapping.
- `vllm/model_executor/models/registry.py` — `RWKV7ForCausalLM -> ("rwkv7", "RWKV7ForCausalLM")`.
- `vllm/transformers_utils/

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix and stabilize EAGLE3 acceptance tests
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
This updates the EAGLE3 acceptance length regression test to use ROCm backend auto-selection instead of hard-selecting attention backends. It adds ROCm-specific expected per-position acceptance values for gpt-oss without widening the default tolerance. The Qwen3-VL FP8 MoE case is limited to ROCm TP4 and enables expert parallelism to avoid the unsupported block-FP8 TP shard shape. The per-position check now treats lower-than-baseline acceptance as the regression signal, so improvements do not fail the test. 

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [Bugfix][ROCm] Fix FP8 per-tensor scale rank mismatch causing Inductor assertion failure
prs.author: nehmathe2
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Fix `AssertionError` in Inductor's aten._scaled_mm lowering due to mismatched scale tensor ranks when AITER is enabled (`VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1`)  and skinny GEMMs are disabled ( `VLLM_ROCM_USE_SKINNY_GEMM=0`) on ROCm.

`requantize_with_max_scale` returns a 0-D  weight scale, but AITER's `_rocm_aiter_per_tensor_quant_fake` always returns a 1-D activation scale. When torch.compile lowers aten._scaled_mm, it asserts that scale_a and scale_b have matching ranks, causing EngineCore to fail on startup.

This patch normalizes both scales to 1-D before they are stored, ensuring consistent ranks at compile time.
## Test Plan
Run vLLM with a static FP8 per-tensor quantized model on ROCm with AITER enabled and skinny GEMMs disabled:

`AMDGCN_USE_BUFFER_OPS=1 VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 VLLM_ROCM_USE_SKINNY_GEMM=0 USE_FASTSAFETENSOR=1 SAFETENSORS_FAST_GPU=1 vllm serve amd/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct-FP8-KV --max-model-len 12000 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.92 --no-enable-prefix-caching --disable-log-stats --kv-cache-dtype fp8 --async-scheduling --attention-backend ROCM_AITER_FA`

Verify the model loads, compiles, and serves requests successfully.
## Test Result
Before:

```
torch._inductor.exc.InductorError: LoweringException: AssertionError:
  target: aten._scaled_mm.default
  File "torch/_inductor/template_heuristics/triton.py", line 1879, in adjust_kernel_inputs
    assert len(scale_a.get_size()) == len(scale_b.get_size())
```

`EngineCore` fails to start during benchmark run

After:

Model loads and compiles successfully and benchmark completes.
Validated on MI355X with `Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct-FP8-KV`.

prs.title: [Bugfix][CI][Hardware][AMD] Fix various e4m3fn -> e4m3fnuz normalization issues
prs.author: mawong-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
This PR fixes various issues in e4m3fnuz handling encountered by various quantization code paths (ModelOpt, online). In particular, on `e4m3fnuz` platforms, `normalize_e4m3fn_to_e4m3fnuz` should be called exactly once on a given FP8 linear layer after weights are loaded.
However,
- Some PyTorch linear kernel-based code paths used during ModelOpt quantization were not calling this function.
- Some code paths used in online quantization methods were trying to call this function twice, once in the linear layer and once more in the linear layer's kernel implementation.

The end result is that the Quantization test group should now be passing on ROCm.

This PR also removes a Quark mxfp4 model that was causing failures in the Kernels MoE test group because it is incompatible with HF Transformers v5.
## Test Plan
`VLLM_TEST_FORCE_LOAD_FORMAT=auto pytest -v -s quantization/ --ignore quantization/test_blackwell_moe.py`
`pytest -v -s kernels/moe --ignore=kernels/moe/test_modular_oai_triton_moe.py`
These tests should be run as part of the Quantization and Kernels MoE test groups in vLLM AMD CI.

## Test Result
The above test groups pass, and there are no regressions.

cc @kenroche and @AndreasKaratzas

---

## Test Result

prs.title: [Bugfix][Rocm]Aiter MoE re-uses existing tensor addresses after weight update.
prs.author: yuankaichen-amd
prs.body_head:
The AMD aiter FusedMoE requires model weights be shuffled before use. When using this feature in RL scenario, however, the MoE weights will be re-shuffled time and again whenever a weight update is carried out.

The issue is that the weight shuffle should NOT change the tensor addresses -- otherwise captured CUDA graph will not be able to use the new weights.

The fix in this PR is to preserve the weights' addresses if they already exist.

## Purpose
Aiter MoE re-uses existing tensor addresses after weight update.

## Test Plan
Test with "export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_MOE=1" in RL setup (e.g. veRL + vLLM, enforce_eager=False).

## Test Result
Without the fix, the generated responses are all gibberish. The fix restores the correctness.

---

prs.title: [Reasoning] Add GLMReasoningParser with embedded tool-call rescue
prs.author: jatseng-ai
prs.body_head:
GLM-4/5-series models served via --reasoning-parser glm45 write tool calls as XML inside <think> blocks. The sequential reasoning-parser to tool-parser pipeline silently drops them in three ways at >8k prompt tokens:

Phase 1 - complete <tool_call>...</tool_call> block captured by reasoning
  parser before the tool parser sees it.
Phase 2 - vLLM strips the <|observation|> special token (id 154829) that
  opens a tool call; body appears in reasoning_content without opening tag.
Phase 3 - closing </arg_value></tool_call> fragment stranded in
  reasoning_content while the rest of the call landed in content.

Fix: add vllm/reasoning/glm_reasoning_parser.py with GLMReasoningParser subclassing DeepSeekV3ReasoningWithThinkingParser, overriding extract_reasoning to run _split_embedded_tool_calls post-extraction. Point glm45 and holo2 aliases at the new class.

Tests: 20 unit tests covering all three phases plus the Phase 1+2 interaction bug. Validated in production at 45k prompt tokens on AMD MI300X (TP=8).




## Purpose

## Test Plan

## Test Result

prs.title: [ROCm] Use quant_dtype in per_token_quant instead of hardcoded FP8
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
`_rocm_aiter_per_token_quant_impl` and its fake both accept `quant_dtype` (which can be `torch.int8` or `FP8_DTYPE` per the assert), but hardcode `FP8_DTYPE` for the output tensor allocation. This means passing `torch.int8` would still get an FP8 tensor back.

Simple fix: use `quant_dtype` instead of `FP8_DTYPE` for `torch.empty()`.

Currently all callers pass `FP8_DTYPE` so this hasn't surfaced, but it's a correctness bug if int8 quantization is ever enabled on this path.

No duplicate PRs found (PR #36092 covers other _aiter_ops bugs but not this one). AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [ROCm][AITER][Bugfix] Disable emulation for MoE
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
PR #39801 introduced a regression for models using the w_mxfp4_a_mxfp4 scheme (e.g. Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4). The _AITER_NATIVE_OCP_MX_SCHEMES tuple only included w_mxfp4, so w_mxfp4_a_mxfp4 was not recognized as a natively supported scheme and fell back to emulation despite aiter's CK MoE kernel supporting it. This PR adds w_mxfp4_a_mxfp4 to the supported set, restoring native execution. 

## Test Plan

[x] Added a unit test to make sure `emulate` is set correctly

## Test Result
